changes_made: No code changes required - all three files reviewed and confirmed to be well-structured

notes: CSS rules are logically ordered: .prose pre (container) -> .prose pre code.hljs (highlighted) -> .prose pre code:not(.hljs) (fallback) -> inline code
Comment 'Fallback for code blocks without language specification (.hljs class absent)' clearly explains the purpose of the new rule
bg-[#0d1117] dark background with text-[#c9d1d9] light text provides good contrast for unspecified-language code blocks
rounded-md and overflow-x-auto on .prose pre ensure consistent visual treatment
prose-pre:bg-gray-100 prose-pre:border prose-pre:border-gray-200 correctly removed - global CSS now handles .prose pre styling
Custom pre component simplified to overflow-x-auto only - background/border/padding/rounded handled by globals.css .prose pre rule
Long className string on line 150 is an existing pattern, not introduced by this change
bg-red-50 on error pre element is a defensive measure - prevents .prose pre { bg-[#0d1117] } from overriding the error styling when MermaidDiagram is rendered within a .prose container
Consistent with parent div bg-red-50 for visual coherence

summary: All three target files were reviewed and confirmed to be well-implemented. No refactoring changes were needed. The CSS changes in globals.css follow a logical ordering with clear comments. The page.tsx correctly delegates pre styling to the global CSS layer. The MermaidDiagram.tsx bg-red-50 addition serves as an important defensive measure against .prose pre dark background override. All 4251 unit tests pass, TypeScript type checking passes, and ESLint reports zero errors.
